steps.title: Identify the modifier rule
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase: "Wanting to celebrate the 100th anniversary of the Alaska Purchase." A participial phrase at the start of a sentence must logically modify the grammatical subject of the clause that follows the comma. Whatever noun comes right after the comma must be capable of "wanting" to celebrate.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — a contest sponsored by the Alaska Centennial Commission would award \$300 to an individual who came: A contest cannot want to celebrate an anniversary — this creates a dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice B — an award of \$300 would go to an individual in a contest sponsored by the Alaska Centennial Commission for coming: An award cannot want to celebrate — another dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice C — \$300 would be awarded to an individual by the Alaska Centennial Commission in a contest for coming: \$300 cannot want to celebrate — dangling modifier again. ✗
  • Choice D — the Alaska Centennial Commission sponsored a contest that would award \$300 to an individual who came: The Alaska Centennial Commission is the entity that logically wanted to celebrate, so placing it as the subject immediately after the modifying phrase is correct. ✓
That is the correct answer.
